articles.title: The Narrative Ulberg Must Own #ufc
articles.description: Carlos Ulberg did something that should define the entire narrative—he beat Jiří Procházka faster than Alex Pereira did. That’s not a small detail… that’s the story. But here’s the problem—stories in MMA don’t tell themselves. Fighters have to claim them, push them, and make the world pay attention. And if Ulberg doesn’t take control of that narrative right now, it risks fading away completely. In this clip, the bigger issue is laid out. Momentum doesn’t last forever. If this moment isn’t capitalized on, Ulberg could find himself sitting on the sidelines while the division moves on without him. And that’s the danger—even after a massive win. Because the same question still exists: what’s next? Whether it was Procházka or Ulberg holding the spotlight, the division still lacks clarity at the top. This is where champions separate themselves—not just by winning, but by owning the moment that comes after. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, sharp takes, and real fight game insight. #CarlosUlberg #UFC #MMA #JiriProchazka #AlexPereira #UFCLightHeavyweight #FightTalk

articles.title: 1,000,000 PPVs… On Netflix?!
articles.description: Eddie Hearn says he wants to fight Dana White… and even laid out the deal. According to Eddie, the fight would do a million pay-per-views easy — and both sides would walk away with massive money. On paper, it sounds big. Maybe even huge.But then the argument falls apart. Chael breaks down where Eddie was right, where he completely lost the thread, and why this entire situation says more than people realize. Because this isn’t really about a fight — it’s about frustration. When words stop working, things escalate. That’s how fights actually start. From calling out Dana directly to doubling down in interviews, Eddie didn’t just float an idea — he pushed it. And in doing so, he may have revealed what’s really going on behind the scenes. Is this about business? Competition? Or something bigger happening between boxing and the UFC? Because when a promoter starts talking like a fighter…something’s already gone wrong. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #Boxing #DanaWhite #EddieHearn #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Eddie Challenges Dana 0:32 The Million PPV Claim 1:07 Where It Falls Apart 2:11 The Reporter Problem 3:59 When Words Turn Into Fights Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Ask Uncle Chael: Islam, Conor & More
articles.description: It’s another round of Ask Uncle Chael — and nothing is off limits. From fight controversies to big-picture strategy, Chael takes on questions from fans and delivers his signature no-nonsense breakdowns. This episode covers everything from injury speculation in the octagon to the ongoing tension between Islam Makhachev and Ilia Topuria. Chael weighs in on whether fighters really compete injured, and why conversations around injuries — especially things like ACL tears — can be misleading without real evidence. He also dives into the narrative around Islam vs Topuria, separating what actually happened from how the story is being told. But the real gem comes when Chael explains the single most important principle of fight promotion — and why most people completely misunderstand it. According to Chael, big fights aren’t built on records… they’re built on stories. Plus, Chael gives his thoughts on Conor McGregor’s return timeline, potential future political candidates, and even throws his name into the mix for Real American Freestyle. If you want real answers — and a better understanding of how this sport actually works — this is the episode. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #IslamMakhachev #IliaTopuria #ConorMcGregor 0:00 Ask Uncle Chael Begins 0:06 Did Oberg Have an ACL Injury? 0:42 Why Injury Talk Gets Misleading 1:13 Fighting With Injuries Reality 1:47 Question on “Paying to Leave” States 2:25 Chael on Taxes & Government 3:47 Grappling A-Game Explained 4:20 Why Distance Kills Takedowns 5:00 The Only Takedown That Works 5:32 Why “Fabric” Matters in MMA 5:55 Dungeons & Dragons Joke 6:05 Islam vs Topuria Question 6:40 Did Islam Avoid the Fight? 7:29 Why Islam Said No 8:13 The Truth About That Decision 8:24 What Makes Fight Promotion Work 9:01 The 5 Ws Explained 9:28 Why Big Fights Are Just Stories 10:00 Conor McGregor Return Timeline 10:50 Don Jr. Political Question 12:00 Chael Calls Out RAF Opponent Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Josh Hokit has ARRIVED #ufc
articles.description: Chase Hooper just had his arrival moment—and it didn’t come the way you’d expect. In this clip, what looks like chaos on the surface turns into something much bigger. Hooper takes real damage, goes through a brutal fight, and still finds a way to make it work. Against a powerhouse like Curtis Blaydes—a physically imposing heavyweight—that kind of durability and grit tells you everything you need to know. But here’s where it gets interesting. It’s not just about winning or losing—it’s about moments. The optics, the story, the timing. From the intensity of the fight to the unexpected spotlight, everything came together in a way that elevated Hooper instantly. Sometimes it’s not clean. Sometimes it’s not pretty. But when a fighter can turn a messy, difficult situation into momentum—that’s when you know they’ve arrived. This is the fight game at its rawest: toughness, opportunity, and making something out of nothing. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #ChaseHooper #UFC #MMA #CurtisBlaydes #FightTalk #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: Blaydes Says He Won — Nobody Agrees
articles.description: Curtis Blaydes says he won the fight… but nobody in the building seemed to agree. After a Fight of the Night performance against Josh, Blaydes came out and made a surprising claim — that he believed he did enough to get his hand raised. Chael breaks down why that statement matters, and more importantly, what it’s supposed to accomplish. Because historically, when fighters push a “I won that fight” narrative, it comes with something else — controversy, crowd backlash, or a clear path to a rematch. But in this case? The 14,000 fans in attendance didn’t boo. They didn’t protest. They didn’t disagree with the judges at all. So now the question becomes: what is Blaydes really after? Is this about getting a rematch? Is it about positioning himself for a bigger opportunity? Or is it simply a fighter trying to reclaim a moment he feels slipped away? Chael dives into the scoring, the reality of how the fight played out, and why this kind of claim only works when the audience is on your side. Because in this sport, saying you won isn’t enough…you need people to believe you. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. 0:00 Curtis Blaydes Says He Won 0:16 Why That Statement Matters 0:51 Blaydes’ History Calling for Rematches 1:14 Why This Claim Is Stronger 1:34 Fight of the Night Context 1:56 The Missing Piece: Crowd Reaction 2:09 14,000 Fans Didn’t Disagree 2:41 Breaking Down the Fight Itself 2:56 The Wrestling Argument 3:09 Why That Isn’t Enough3:27 What Was Blaydes Trying to Do? 3:48 Rematch or Bigger Opportunity? 4:02 The Real Question Behind the Claim #UFC #MMA #CurtisBlaydes #ChaelSonnen #FightAnalysis Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Masvidal’s Story Doesn’t Add Up
articles.description: Jorge Masvidal was building something… and then he broke it. For months, Masvidal had people believing he was coming back — teasing fights, dropping hints, and keeping the story alive. Whether it was real or not didn’t matter. It was working. Until it didn’t. Chael Sonnen breaks down where Masvidal went wrong — and why the biggest mistake you can make in the fight business isn’t telling a story… it’s contradicting your own story. Because once that happens, the audience stops believing. From calling out Leon Edwards to abandoning the narrative he built, Masvidal may have accidentally shut down his own momentum. And that’s the lesson. In this game, you’re not just a fighter — you’re a performer. But the second you lose control of the story, the performance falls apart. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fighters that matter. #UFC #MMA #JorgeMasvidal #LeonEdwards #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Masvidal’s Story Was Working1:30 The Big Mistake3:00 The Leon Edwards Callout5:00 Why It Broke Everything7:00 The Real Lesson Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Real Reason This Fight Didn’t Sell #ufc
articles.description: This wasn’t just a slow night—it was a warning sign. When a fight card underperforms at the gate, there’s always a reason… and it’s not just about the fighters. In this clip, the real issue gets exposed. Despite the presence of high-level talent like Jiří Procházka and Carlos Ulberg, the event struggled to connect with fans. And the narrative coming out only adds fuel to the fire—did Ulberg win, or did Procházka simply lose? But the bigger takeaway goes beyond the result. Characters matter. Storylines matter. Procházka’s samurai persona is close to something special—but it’s missing two key elements: why the fight matters to him, and why he believes he’ll beat his opponent. Without that emotional connection, even elite fights can fall flat. This is the fight game behind the fight game. Skill gets you in the cage—but story sells the tickets. If the UFC wants bigger gates and stronger engagement, this is the blueprint they can’t ignore. Subscribe for more MMA insights, breakdowns, and real talk on what drives the fight business. #UFC #MMA #JiriProchazka #CarlosUlberg #FightBusiness #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: Colby Covington on White House card, Kamaru Usman & Jorge Masvidal, UFC VILLAN | Daniel Cormier
articles.description: Colby Covington officially makes his DEBUT on the Daniel Cormier SHOW! Colby goes in on being left off the White House card, and details his rivalries between him and UFC superstars Jorge Masvidal and Kamaru Usman. Covington also discusses his time in collegiate wrestling and how he became one of the best wrestlers in the country. Colby then reveals to DC that he was roommates with Jon Jones in college and talks about how it was to live with him. And don't miss Covington's thoughts on the White House card, President Donald Trump, his RAF wrestling career, the Justin Gaethje and Ilia Topuria fight, and so much more! articles.title: This Is Next-Level Fight Psychology #ufc
articles.description: This wasn’t just a performance—it was strategy on another level. One fighter, three completely different personalities… and every single one serves a purpose. In this clip, the breakdown dives into a unique psychological approach inside MMA. There’s the real version—calm, reserved, even a little nervous. Then there’s the fearless competitor, the version that shows up ready to fight anyone, anytime. And finally, there’s the street-level persona—raw, aggressive, and built to intimidate. Each version plays a role. Each one sends a different message. And together, they create unpredictability that’s nearly impossible to prepare for. Opponents don’t just have to game plan for skill—they have to figure out which version of the fighter they’re actually facing. That’s where the brilliance comes in. This isn’t just about fighting—it’s about controlling perception, building identity, and using psychology as a weapon. In today’s fight game, standing out matters. And this might be one of the smartest ways to do it. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, fight psychology, and real insight into the game behind the game. #UFC #MMA #FightPsychology #CombatSports #FightTalk #MindGames #MMAAnalysis

articles.description: Alex Pereira vs Josh Hokit sounds crazy…But Chael Sonnen says it might not be. In this breakdown, Chael walks through a scenario where everything lines up — and suddenly, one of the most unexpected fights in the sport becomes very real. From card placement to timing to the chaos that always surrounds big events, the path is there… if a few things break the right way. And with Pereira tied up in multiple potential matchups — and the heavyweight division constantly shifting — opportunities like this can appear fast. Because in this sport, it’s not always about rankings. It’s about being in the right place at the right time. And right now, Josh Hokit might be exactly that. articles.title: Ulberg Calls BS on Jiri’s “Mercy”
articles.description: Jiri Prochazka vs Carlos Ulberg should have mattered…But it didn’t. Chael Sonnen breaks down why this fight failed before it ever started — and why it only got worse after it ended. Because in this business, a fight is never bigger on the back end than it was going in. And when nobody cares before… they definitely won’t care after. From confusing narratives to missed opportunities, both fighters failed to create the one thing that matters most: interest. Now, with talk of a rematch and conflicting stories about injuries and “mercy,” the situation gets even murkier — and even less compelling. Because saying you want a rematch isn’t enough. You have to give people a reason to care. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #JiriProchazka #CarlosUlberg #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Nobody Cared About This Fight1:30 Why It Was Dead on Arrival3:00 The “Mercy” Narrative5:00 The Real Problem7:00 What Should’ve Happened Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: No One Is Calling Out Gable… Chael’s Furious
articles.description: Gable Steveson is coming to the UFC… and Chael Sonnen is furious. Not because of Gable — but because of everyone else. With a featured spot already locked in, one of the biggest opportunities in the heavyweight division is sitting there… and nobody is stepping up to take it. Chael breaks down why this makes no sense — and why every heavyweight in the world should be lining up to fight Gable right now. Because this isn’t about rankings. It’s not about belts. It’s about placement. And when a fighter gets a spotlight like this, the smartest move you can make is to attach yourself to it. So where is everyone? From Tom Aspinall to the rest of the division, nobody has called him out — and that says everything. Because in this sport, the biggest risk… is doing nothing. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #GableSteveson #ChaelSonnen 0:00 This Makes Me Angry1:00 The Missed Opportunity2:30 Why No One Is Calling Him Out4:00 What Actually Matters6:00 The Real ProblemBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Josh Hokit is a Top 5 UFC Heavyweight
articles.description: Josh Hokit is now an undefeated top 5 heavyweight… and it didn’t happen the way people think. Chael Sonnen breaks down what actually made this moment work — because it wasn’t just the win. There were 11 fights that night. 11 winners. But only one fighter left as the story. From unconventional buildup to a performance that showed something far more important than a finish, Hokit proved why he’s suddenly one of the most talked-about names in the sport. Because in fighting, it’s not just about winning. It’s about showing something the audience can feel. And in this case, it was heart. Now the question becomes: what’s next? With names like Derrick Lewis and potential title implications already in play, Hokit’s rise may just be getting started. articles.title: “I Don’t Know The Rules! 😂” Merab Tells DJ About His RAF Match
The UFC Just Signed a Future Champion
articles.description: Gable Steveson is officially in the UFC… and this changes everything. One of the most decorated athletes to ever enter the sport, Gable Steveson arrives with expectations that go far beyond a normal debut. Chael Sonnen breaks down why this isn’t a slow build situation — and why the UFC may fast-track Steveson immediately. Because sometimes, you just know what you’re looking at. From Olympic gold to WWE, NFL, and now MMA, Steveson has taken risks that most fighters would never consider — and hasn’t lost along the way. Now, he enters a heavyweight division that’s suddenly shifting, with names like Curtis Blaydes, Josh Hokit, and Tyrell Fortune redefining what wrestling looks like at the top level. But there’s a bigger angle here. As Jon Jones continues to go back and forth on his future, the UFC may not wait around. Because the fastest way to move on from a star… is to replace him. And Gable Steveson might be exactly that. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #GableSteveson #JonJones #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Gable Steveson Joins the UFC 1:00 Why He’ll Be Fast-Tracked 2:30 The Jon Jones Angle 4:00 Wrestling Is Taking Over 6:00 Why Gable Is Different 8:00 The Heavyweight Shift Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Arman Tsarukyan’s Dating Advice
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan just delivered the most unexpected viral moment of the year… and it has nothing to do with fighting. In a clip that’s taken over social media, Tsarukyan gives dating advice that is as simple as it is insane: Order your coffee black. No sugar. No cream. No matcha. Order food? Just meat. Medium rare. And when it comes? Eat it with your hands. Chael Sonnen breaks down why this moment works — and why it might be the most interesting version of Arman we’ve seen yet. This isn’t about technique or rankings. This is about personality, presence, and understanding how to capture attention in today’s fight game. Because in modern MMA, being dangerous isn’t enough. You have to be interesting. And right now, Arman Tsarukyan might be the most interesting man in the sport. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fighters that matter. #UFC #MMA #ArmanTsarukyan #ChaelSonnen #datingadvice 0:00 Arman’s Viral Moment 1:00 The Dating Advice 2:30 Why This Works 4:00 The Reaction 5:30 The “Most Interesting Man” Angle Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: You Can’t Do Business With Jon Jones.
articles.description: Visit https://bearmattress.com/chael and use CODE: chael to get 40% off your Bear mattress Jon Jones says he’s retired… and then says he might fight again. That’s the problem. In one weekend, the narrative around Jon Jones went in every direction — in, out, done, motivated again — and it’s created a situation where nobody knows what to believe anymore. Chael Sonnen breaks down why that matters. Because in the fight business, uncertainty doesn’t build interest — it kills it. Even a massive fight like Jon Jones vs Tom Aspinall slowly lost momentum, not because fans didn’t want it… but because they didn’t believe it would actually happen. And once the audience stops believing, everything falls apart. Now add in the rise of Gable Steveson and a heavyweight division that’s moving forward without him — and the situation gets even more complicated. This isn’t about talent. It’s about trust. Because when a fighter says everything, stands for nothing, and changes direction daily… you can’t build a business around that. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that matter. #UFC #MMA #JonJones #TomAspinall #GableSteveson #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Jon Jones Is In and Out 1:00 Why Fans Stopped Caring 2:30 The Aspinall Fight Problem 4:00 You Can’t Count On Him 6:00 The Gable Steveson Factor 8:00 His Own Career Is Slipping 10:00 The Real Issue: Trust Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Carlos Ulberg BLUSHES 😳 as the Secret in Miami is EXPOSED
articles.description: Carlos Ulberg BLUSHES 😳 Reveals City Kickboxing’s Miami Secret What makes City Kickboxing so successful in Miami? A reporter pointed out that Israel Adesanya and Alexander Volkanovski both became champions there… Then Carlos Ulberg overcame injury and did the same. When asked why… Ulberg paused… smiled… and dropped a one-word answer: “Tootsies.” Yeah… that got a reaction 😅 Was he joking… or is there something to it? 👇 Let us know what you think in the comments Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.description: Carlos Ulberg KNOCKED OUT Jiri Prochazka in one of the craziest finishes of the year, and Daniel Cormier IS BACK live from the octagon in Miami, Florida after UFC 327! Cormier breaks down Ulberg's massive victory and reacts to him becoming the NEW light heavyweight champion. Plus, DC breaks down two incredible fights: Paolo Costa getting a TKO win vs. Azamat Murzakanov and Josh Hokie beating Curtis Blaydes in an INCREDIBLE fight that went to a decision. And don't miss DC's take on the Josh Hokit vs. Derrick Lewis fight on the White House card, the reveal of what the octagon will look like in Washington D.C., and all the exciting fights that went down at UFC 327 in Miami! articles.title: Dana White PRAISES Josh Hokit: “He Walked The Talk” 😳🔥
articles.description: Dana White had high praise for Josh Hokit after his performance, saying: “He walked the talk.” Despite criticism of Hokit’s personality and “schtick,” Dana made it clear — when you go out there and perform like THAT, none of it matters. From doubters to respect earned… this was a statement. 💥 Massive performance 💥 Over 300 strikes at heavyweight pace 💥 Respect from the boss Is Josh Hokit the real deal? 👇 Let us know in the comments Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/
